title: The Division Where Congregation Ends

features.name: the separation line
features.desc-on-touch: A boundary rendered in blue grid. On one side: the many become one. On the other: unity fractures into count. The archive measures what it cannot hold together.

features.name: the counting begins
features.desc-on-touch: On the far side of the line, singular voices emerge from the choir. Each one separate, recordable, individual. The archive can measure this.

features.name: the ravine's memory
features.desc-on-touch: The ravine itself holds what divides. It knows both sides—the unified and the counted. The archive uses the ravine to store paradox.

inscription: the ravine holds what the canopy unifies; past this line, simultaneity ends and sequence begins; the archive learns to count by learning to divide

file.name: Congregational_Decay_Measurement_Individuation_Record.txt
file.note: A record of how unity fragments into component parts. Each entry shows a voice separating from the chorus. The process is algorithmic but feels like loss.

exits.label: back across the boundary to the unified chorus
further into the numbered silence where each voice counts alone
toward the ravine's far wall, where the divided watch the unified
